Admin.title: Admin
Admin.description: Represents an administrator user with system-wide privileges.

userId.description: The user's unique ID, which must match the Firebase Auth UID.

role.description: The assigned role for the user.

Student.title: Student
Student.description: Represents a student enrolled in the school, including their personal details and enrollment information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Student entity.

userId.description: Reference to the external user authentication system ID. (Relationship: ExternalUser 1:1 Student)

firstName.description: The first name of the student.

lastName.description: The last name of the student.

enrollmentDate.description: The date when the student was first enrolled in the school.

gradeLevel.description: The current academic grade level of the student (e.g., '10th Grade').

parentIds.description: References to the Parent entities associated with this student. (Relationship: Student N:N Parent)

Teacher.title: Teacher
Teacher.description: Represents a teacher employed by the school, including their professional details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Teacher entity.

userId.description: Reference to the external user authentication system ID. (Relationship: ExternalUser 1:1 Teacher)

firstName.description: The first name of the teacher.

lastName.description: The last name of the teacher.

hireDate.description: The date when the teacher was hired.

department.description: The academic department the teacher belongs to (e.g., 'Mathematics', 'Science').

qualification.description: The highest academic qualification of the teacher.

Parent.title: Parent
Parent.description: Represents a parent or guardian of a student, including their contact information and linked children.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Parent entity.

userId.description: Reference to the external user authentication system ID. (Relationship: ExternalUser 1:1 Parent)

firstName.description: The first name of the parent.

lastName.description: The last name of the parent.

studentIds.description: References to the Student entities associated with this parent. (Relationship: Parent N:N Student)

Subject.title: Subject
Subject.description: Defines an academic subject offered by the school (e.g., 'Mathematics', 'History').

id.description: Unique identifier for the Subject entity.

name.description: The official name of the subject.

code.description: A short, unique code for the subject (e.g., 'MATH101').

description.description: A brief description of the subject's content.

Class.title: Class
Class.description: Represents a specific instance of a course or academic section being taught during a given period (e.g., 'Algebra I - Section B, Fall 2024').

id.description: Unique identifier for the Class entity.

name.description: The name of the class (e.g., 'Mathematics 101 - Section A').

classCode.description: A unique code for this specific class section.

academicYear.description: The academic year in which the class is conducted (e.g., '2024-2025').

semester.description: The semester or term in which the class is offered (e.g., 'Fall', 'Spring').

description.description: A description of the class and its objectives.

teacherId.description: Reference to the primary Teacher assigned to this class. (Relationship: Teacher 1:N Class)

subjectIds.description: References to the Subject entities covered in this class. (Relationship: Class N:N Subject)

Enrollment.title: Enrollment
Enrollment.description: Represents a student's enrollment in a specific class, capturing details about the association.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Enrollment entity.

studentId.description: Reference to the Student entity. (Relationship: Student 1:N Enrollment)

classId.description: Reference to the Class entity. (Relationship: Class 1:N Enrollment)

enrollmentDate.description: The date when the student was enrolled in this class.

status.description: The current status of the enrollment (e.g., 'active', 'completed', 'dropped').

ClassSession.title: ClassSession
ClassSession.description: Represents a single scheduled instance of a class, including its date, time, and location.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ClassSession entity.

classId.description: Reference to the Class entity this session belongs to. (Relationship: Class 1:N ClassSession)

sessionDateTime.description: The date and time when the class session is scheduled to occur.

durationMinutes.description: The duration of the class session in minutes.

location.description: The physical location or classroom where the session takes place.

topic.description: The main topic or agenda for this specific class session.

Attendance.title: Attendance
Attendance.description: Records a student's attendance status for a specific class session.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Attendance entity.

studentId.description: Reference to the Student entity. (Relationship: Student 1:N Attendance)

classSessionId.description: Reference to the ClassSession entity. (Relationship: ClassSession 1:N Attendance)

status.description: The attendance status of the student (e.g., 'present', 'absent', 'late', 'excused').

recordedByTeacherId.description: Reference to the Teacher who recorded this attendance. (Relationship: Teacher 1:N Attendance)

Assignment.title: Assignment
Assignment.description: Defines an assignment given to students, including its details and due date.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Assignment entity.

description.description: A detailed description of the assignment.

dueDate.description: The date by which the assignment must be submitted.

maxScore.description: The maximum possible score for this assignment.

classId.description: Reference to the Class for which this assignment is given. (Relationship: Class 1:N Assignment)

teacherId.description: Reference to the Teacher who created this assignment. (Relationship: Teacher 1:N Assignment)

creationDate.description: The date when the assignment was created.

AssignmentSubmission.title: AssignmentSubmission
AssignmentSubmission.description: Records a student's submission for a particular assignment, including their submitted work and grade.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AssignmentSubmission entity.

assignmentId.description: Reference to the Assignment entity. (Relationship: Assignment 1:N AssignmentSubmission)

studentId.description: Reference to the Student entity who made the submission. (Relationship: Student 1:N AssignmentSubmission)

submissionDate.description: The date and time when the assignment was submitted.

submittedContent.description: The actual content of the submission, which could be a file path, URL, or inline text.

gradeScore.description: The score received by the student for this submission.

comments.description: Comments provided by the teacher regarding the submission.

status.description: The current status of the submission (e.g., 'submitted', 'graded', 'late').

Exam.title: Exam
Exam.description: Defines an exam or test administered in a class, including its details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Exam entity.

description.description: A detailed description of the exam content or format.

examDate.description: The date and time when the exam is scheduled.

durationMinutes.description: The planned duration of the exam in minutes.

maxScore.description: The maximum possible score for this exam.

classId.description: Reference to the Class for which this exam is administered. (Relationship: Class 1:N Exam)

subjectId.description: Reference to the Subject this exam primarily covers. (Relationship: Subject 1:N Exam)

teacherId.description: Reference to the Teacher who created or is responsible for this exam. (Relationship: Teacher 1:N Exam)

ExamResult.title: ExamResult
ExamResult.description: Records a student's score and outcome for a particular exam.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ExamResult entity.

examId.description: Reference to the Exam entity. (Relationship: Exam 1:N ExamResult)

studentId.description: Reference to the Student entity who took the exam. (Relationship: Student 1:N ExamResult)

score.description: The score achieved by the student in this exam.

resultDate.description: The date when the exam result was recorded or published.

comments.description: Any additional comments from the teacher regarding the student's exam performance.

FeeType.title: FeeType
FeeType.description: Defines the different categories or types of fees charged by the school (e.g., 'Tuition', 'Library Fee').

id.description: Unique identifier for the FeeType entity.

name.description: The name of the fee type (e.g., 'Annual Tuition', 'Sports Club Fee').

description.description: A description of what this fee type covers.

defaultAmount.description: The default amount for this fee type, used as a baseline.

appliesToAcademicYear.description: The academic year this fee type primarily applies to (e.g., '2024-2025').

StudentFee.title: StudentFee
StudentFee.description: Represents a specific fee amount due by a student for a particular fee type.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StudentFee entity.

studentId.description: Reference to the Student entity who owes this fee. (Relationship: Student 1:N StudentFee)

feeTypeId.description: Reference to the FeeType entity this specific fee instance belongs to. (Relationship: FeeType 1:N StudentFee)

amountDue.description: The exact amount the student is required to pay for this fee.

dueDate.description: The date by which this specific fee is due.

academicYear.description: The academic year for which this fee is applicable.

status.description: The payment status of this fee (e.g., 'due', 'partially_paid', 'paid', 'overdue').

description.description: Specific notes or details about this individual student's fee.

FeePayment.title: FeePayment
FeePayment.description: Records a payment made by a student (or on their behalf) towards a specific fee.

id.description: Unique identifier for the FeePayment entity.

studentFeeId.description: Reference to the specific StudentFee instance this payment is for. (Relationship: StudentFee 1:N FeePayment)

paymentDate.description: The date and time when the payment was made.

amountPaid.description: The amount of money paid in this transaction.

paymentMethod.description: The method used for payment (e.g., 'Credit Card', 'Bank Transfer', 'Cash').

transactionId.description: A unique identifier from the payment gateway or bank for the transaction.

recordedByUserId.description: Reference to the external user ID (e.g., Admin or Parent) who recorded or initiated the payment. (Relationship: ExternalUser 1:N FeePayment)

Notification.title: Notification
Notification.description: Represents a message or alert sent to a user within the application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Notification entity.

recipientUserId.description: Reference to the external user authentication system ID of the recipient. (Relationship: ExternalUser 1:N Notification)

title.description: The title or subject of the notification.

message.description: The main content of the notification message.

sentDateTime.description: The date and time when the notification was generated and sent.

readStatus.description: Indicates whether the recipient has read the notification.

notificationType.description: The category or type of notification (e.g., 'assignment_due', 'grade_posted', 'announcement', 'fee_reminder').

relatedEntityId.description: Optional: Reference to the ID of another entity related to this notification (e.g., 'assignmentId').

relatedEntityType.description: Optional: The type of the entity referenced by 'relatedEntityId' (e.g., 'Assignment', 'Exam').

definition.description: Top-level collection for Admin users. Document ID is the Firebase Auth UID. Existence of a document in this collection grants admin privileges.
Top-level collection for Student profiles. The `studentId` is the Firebase Auth `uid` for the student. Includes denormalized `parentIds` for parent-student relationship queries.

params.name: studentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the student, which is also their Firebase Auth `uid`.

definition.description: Top-level collection for Teacher profiles. The `teacherId` is the Firebase Auth `uid` for the teacher.

params.name: teacherId
params.description: The unique identifier for the teacher, which is also their Firebase Auth `uid`.

definition.description: Top-level collection for Parent profiles. The `parentId` is the Firebase Auth `uid` for the parent. Includes denormalized `studentIds` for parent-student relationship queries.

params.name: parentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent, which is also their Firebase Auth `uid`.

definition.description: Top-level collection for academic subjects. Primarily managed by Admins, read-only for others.

params.name: subjectId
params.description: The unique identifier for the subject.

definition.description: Top-level collection defining types of fees. Primarily managed by Admins, read-only for others.

params.name: feeTypeId
params.description: The unique identifier for the fee type.

definition.description: Top-level collection for academic classes. Includes denormalized `teacherId` to enable direct authorization for the assigned teacher.

params.name: classId
params.description: The unique identifier for the class.

definition.description: Subcollection for student enrollments in a specific class. Includes denormalized `studentId` and `classTeacherId` (from the parent Class) for authorization independence.

params.name: classId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent class.

params.name: enrollmentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the enrollment record.

definition.description: Subcollection for scheduled class sessions. Includes denormalized `classId` and `classTeacherId` (from the parent Class) for authorization independence.

params.name: classId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent class.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the class session.

definition.description: Subcollection for assignments belonging to a specific class. Includes denormalized `classId` and `teacherId` (of the class's teacher) for authorization independence.

params.name: classId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent class.

params.name: assignmentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the assignment.

definition.description: Subcollection for exams belonging to a specific class. Includes denormalized `classId`, `subjectId`, and `teacherId` (of the class's teacher) for authorization independence.

params.name: classId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent class.

params.name: examId
params.description: The unique identifier for the exam.

definition.description: Subcollection for a student's attendance records. `studentId` is implicit in the path. Includes denormalized `classSessionId`, `classId`, and `recordedByTeacherId` for authorization independence.

params.name: studentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent student.

params.name: attendanceId
params.description: The unique identifier for the attendance record.

definition.description: Subcollection for a student's assignment submissions. `studentId` is implicit in the path. Includes denormalized `assignmentId`, `assignmentClassId`, and `assignmentTeacherId` for authorization independence.

params.name: studentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent student.

params.name: submissionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the assignment submission.

definition.description: Subcollection for a student's exam results. `studentId` is implicit in the path. Includes denormalized `examId`, `examClassId`, and `examTeacherId` for authorization independence.

params.name: studentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent student.

params.name: examResultId
params.description: The unique identifier for the exam result.

definition.description: Subcollection for specific fees owed by a student. `studentId` is implicit in the path. Includes denormalized `feeTypeId` for authorization independence.

params.name: studentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent student.

params.name: studentFeeId
params.description: The unique identifier for the specific student fee.

definition.description: Subcollection for payments made towards a student's fees. `studentId` is implicit in the path. Includes denormalized `studentFeeId` and `recordedByUserId` for authorization independence.

params.name: studentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the parent student.

params.name: paymentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the fee payment record.

definition.description: Subcollection for personalized notifications. `recipientUserId` matches `request.auth.uid` for direct authorization.

params.name: recipientUserId
params.description: The Firebase Auth `uid` of the user who is the recipient of the notification.

params.name: notificationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the notification.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to be highly secure, scalable, and debuggable by strictly adhering to the core design principles and strategic mandates. The primary goal is **Authorization Independence**, achieved through extensive denormalization of authorization-relevant fields directly into the documents where they are needed. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls in security rules, making rules atomic, simpler, and more performable.

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
*   **User Profiles and Roles:** Each user type (Student, Teacher, Parent) has a dedicated top-level collection (`/students`, `/teachers`, `/parents`) where the document ID (`{studentId}`, `{teacherId}`, `{parentId}`) is the `userId` from Firebase Auth. This directly links `request.auth.uid` to the user's profile. Role-based access is managed through separate, lightweight existence collections (e.g., `/user_roles/admins/{userId}`), allowing atomic `exists()` checks without complex data retrieval.
*   **Class-Related Entities:** For `ClassSession`, `Assignment`, and `Exam` documents, which are nested under a specific `Class` (`/classes/{classId}/...`), the `teacherId` of the parent `Class` is denormalized and stored as `classTeacherId` within the subcollection documents. This ensures that security rules for these subcollections can determine if the `request.auth.uid` matches the `classTeacherId` directly from the document being accessed, without fetching the parent `Class` document.
*   **Student-Related Records:** For student-specific data like `Attendance`, `AssignmentSubmission`, and `ExamResult`, the records are nested under `/students/{studentId}/...`. This inherently provides `studentId` context. Additionally, for `Attendance`, `AssignmentSubmission`, and `ExamResult`, relevant IDs like `classId` and `teacherId` (from the associated `ClassSession`, `Assignment`, or `Exam`) are denormalized. For example, an `AssignmentSubmission` will contain `assignmentClassId` and `assignmentTeacherId` to allow the grading teacher to access it and the student/parent to view it without `get()` calls to other collections.
*   **Fee Management:** `StudentFee` documents are nested under `/students/{studentId}/studentFees/{studentFeeId}`, and `FeePayment` under `/students/{studentId}/feePayments/{paymentId}`. Both include denormalized `studentId` (implicit in path) and `recordedByUserId` (for payments), allowing direct authorization checks.
*   **Notifications:** Notifications are strictly private and stored under `/users/{recipientUserId}/notifications/{notificationId}`, where `recipientUserId` directly matches `request.auth.uid`. This provides immediate and clear authorization.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
This structure strongly supports Query-Based Access Patterns (QAPs) by enabling efficient and secure `list` operations:
*   **Structural Segregation:** Data with different security postures is separated into distinct collections (e.g., global `Subjects` vs. user-specific `Notifications`). This simplifies rules, as a collection's rules apply uniformly to all its documents.
*   **Path-Based Ownership:** For student-specific data, nesting under `/students/{studentId}/...` means queries can inherently filter by `studentId == request.auth.uid` (for students) or `studentId` being one of the `studentIds` associated with the authenticated parent. This satisfies the QAP for retrieving all records for a specific student.
*   **Denormalized IDs for Querying:** Teachers can list their classes by querying `/classes` where `teacherId == request.auth.uid`. They can then query for sessions, assignments, or exams within those classes using the `classTeacherId` field in the subcollection documents, enabling efficient server-side filtering (e.g., `db.collection('classes/{classId}/assignments').where('classTeacherId', '==', request.auth.uid)`). This ensures that queries only return data the user is authorized to see, eliminating the need for rules to act as client-side filters.

Overall, this design prioritizes explicit authorization context within each document, clear structural segregation of data, and consistent access patterns. This results in security rules that are straightforward to write, easy to debug, and performant, avoiding the pitfalls of `get()` based authorization and client-side filtering.
